citations.quote: Rent: £4,686.18 Total Rent (including £500 Advance Rent Payment) £28.23 Per Day
The subsequent monthly sums due will be shown on the rent schedule to be provided separately to the Tenant.
The Landlord acknowledges the Tenant has paid the Advance Payment of FIVE HUNDRED POUNDS (£500) STERLING which will be credited to the Tenant's rent account.
If the Tenant's Rent instalments account falls into arrears, the Landlord will be entitled to seek payment from the Tenant's guarantor
Where a Rent account is in arrears by two monthly instalments, the Landlord will be entitled to issue a notice to quit
If you accept the Tenancy Agreement but don't move into the Accommodation, you remain financially liable for the Accommodation fees for the full period

findings.text: Total rent for the tenancy is £4,686.18, including a £500 advance rent payment, at a daily rate of £28.23.
The rent schedule showing monthly instalment amounts is not included in the agreement; it is stated to be provided separately.
The tenant paid a £500 advance rent payment, which is credited to the rent account.
The agreement provides for guarantor recourse, notice to quit after two months' arrears, and continued liability if the tenant does not occupy or moves out early.

proposed_artifacts.content:
## Rent Schedule and Payment Records Search for NAP-SRC-0010

**Source Reviewed:** NAP-SRC-0010 (Student Licence Agreement for Miss Anfal Elbushra)

### Key Findings
- Total rent: £4,686.18 (including £500 advance payment).
- The rent schedule is explicitly stated to be provided separately (clause 2(a)).
- No payment records, receipts, or arrears statements are contained in this source.
- The agreement references a guarantor and a notice-to-quit process for arrears.

### Gaps
- The separate rent schedule document has not been located.
- No payment history or account statements are available.
- Current arrears status is unknown.

### Recommended Next Steps
1. Search for the separate rent schedule document (may be a separate PDF or email).
2. Locate payment records or account statements from the university accommodation office.
3. Identify any guarantor documentation or correspondence regarding arrears.
proposed_artifacts.title: Rent Schedule and Payment Records Search Result

proposed_tasks.instructions: Search the matter for any document titled or referencing 'rent schedule', 'payment schedule', or 'instalment plan' associated with source NAP-SRC-0010 (Miss Anfal Elbushra). Also search for payment receipts, account statements, or arrears notices. If found, extract the monthly instalment amounts, due dates, and any payment history. If not found, propose a task to request these records from the university accommodation office.
proposed_tasks.title: Locate separate rent schedule and payment records for NAP-SRC-0010

risk_flags.text: Missing rent schedule prevents verification of monthly instalment amounts and due dates, which are essential for arrears calculation.
The agreement's liability clause (2(e)) may impose full rent even if the tenant never occupied; this could be challenged depending on occupancy facts.
The notice-to-quit provision (2(b)) allows termination after two months' arrears; without payment records, it is unclear if this threshold has been reached.

summary: Reviewed NAP-SRC-0010 (Student Licence) for rent schedule and payment records. The agreement states total rent of £4,686.18, an advance payment of £500, and monthly instalments by recurring card payments, but the rent schedule is explicitly described as provided separately. No payment records, receipts, or arrears statements were found in the source. A follow-up task is proposed to locate the separate rent schedule and any payment history.

uncertainties.text: The separate rent schedule referenced in clause 2(a) is not present in source NAP-SRC-0010; its contents (monthly amounts, due dates) are unknown.
No payment records, receipts, or account statements are available in the provided source to confirm whether the advance payment was actually received or whether any subsequent instalments were paid.
No evidence of arrears, guarantor demand, or notice to quit is present in the source; the current payment status of the tenant is unknown.
